creator: mw@wzff.de
text:
My system has been crashing in ip6 on 9.0-RC1 from a few days ago while
	compiling sbcl in a jail, and since I had lost the kernel debugging
	information and wanted to make sure it wasn't my fault, I upgraded to 9.0-RC2
	This time, it crashed in fork1, again with a page fault.
	
	At first, the jail had these limits, on which sbcl wouldn't compile, because
	it likes to mmap() a huge chunk of memory, which it doesn't completely use.

	jail:foo:memoryuse:deny=2g
	jail:foo:swapuse:deny=2g
	jail:foo:maxproc:deny=4096
	jail:foo:openfiles:deny=4096

	After using rctl -a jail:foo:swapuse:deny=10G, sbcl *seemed* to work, but
	then the server crashed both times. Since rctl is fairly new, I expect the
	bug to be there, but it could be something else entirely. I'm not exactly a
	kernel debugging expert, so I might need further instructions.

	The second time, it didn't generate a nice crash report, but I can at least
	provide a kgdb backtrace, and probably more if you tell me how.

How-To-Repeat: 	Probably by compiling sbcl in a jail with the rctl limits mentioned above.
